name: Agent-First UI
synopsis: A UI protocol that lets a tool describe its interface as data instead of code — so a trusted host you own renders the screens and actions, and nothing the tool sends can run on your machine.
intent: Define a JSON-compatible AFUI protocol that carries no JavaScript, CSS, HTML, WASM, build scripts, callbacks, shell commands, or remote component URLs, so showing a human an operation surface stays data-only

Separate producer, host, and composer roles: producers emit snapshots and patches, trusted hosts render and gate user intent, and composition tools combine ordinary snapshots by namespacing ids/actions/state without adding a new wire type
Provide language-neutral protocol assets plus a lightweight Rust SDK under rust/ for wire types, validation, binding, patching, action input assembly, replay/reporting, and producer/handler tests
mutations: Initial release of the Agent-First UI protocol: producers describe current state and the actions available on it as plain data, and a user-owned trusted host renderer draws the interface and reports user intent back — no producer-supplied UI code runs
Provide the canonical, serde-serializable Rust type definitions for the protocol (state surfaces, actions, intents) so any producer or host renderer interoperates against one shared schema
